content.full_text:
CITY OF WHITTIER

13230 PENN STREET, WHITTIER, CALIFORNIA 90602-1772 (213) 945-5200

Mr. John Wallace
Fair Political Practices Commission
Sacramento, Calif

November 16, 1990

Dear Mr. Wallace:

Thank you for your inquiries concerning the conflict of interest
question posed by this office on behalf of councilmember HENDERSON.

In response to your questions, the following is offered:

Ms. Munoz maintains no ownership interest in Speciality Vehicles.

Speciality Vehicles was the low bidder. By law, unless they are an
unqualified bidder, we are required to accept the lowest bid, therefore
Speciality Vehicles will receive the contract.

At this point in time, we are unsure if Ms. Munoz receives a
commission or will receive a commission as a result of this transaction.
We will attempt to make that determination.

Thank You for your professional assistance.

Sincerely,

Richard Jones
City Attorney

Bob Allen
Law Clerk to Mr. Jones

VICTOR A. LOPEZ          THOMAS K. SAWYER          GENE H. CHANDLER          MYRON D. CLAXTON          ROBERT F. WOEHRMANN          THOMAS G. MAUK
MAYOR                    MAYOR PRO TEM             COUNCILMAN                COUNCILMAN                COUNCILMAN                 CITY MANAGER

NOU 14 '90 11:14 FROM JONES.JONES.ANDERSON PAGE.002

CITY OF WHITTIER

13230 PENN STREET, WHITTIER, CALIFORNIA 90602-1772 (213) 945-8200
TO BE FAXED 916-327-2026

JENNIFER
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
SACRAMENTO, CALIF                          NOVEMBER 14, 1990

DEAR JENNIFER:

PER OUR CONVERSATION A FEW MINUTES AGO REGARDING THE QUESTION OF
WHETHER OR NOT A CONFLICT OF INTEREST EXIST, THE FACTS ARE AS
FOLLOWS:

THE CITY OF WHITTIER WILL AT SOME POINT IN THE FUTURE AWARD A BID
TO PURCHASE (05) TROLLEYS FOR USE IN THE RAPID TRANSIT
PROGRAM. THE LOWBIDDER THUS FAR IS A COMPANY CALLED SPECIALTY
VEHICLES.

COUNCIL WILL BE ASKED IN THE NEAR FUTURE TO VOTE ON APPROVAL OF
THAT CONTRACT AND AWARD TO THE LOW BIDDER THE CONTRACT TO PURCHASE
FROM THEM THE FIVE (05) VEHICLES.

THE SALES REPRESENTATIVE FOR THE COMPANY IS A MS. NANCY MUNOZ
WHO HAS BEEN ASSIGNED THIS ACCOUNT BY THE COMPANY. MS. MUNOZ
ALSO PURCHASES INSURANCE FROM A COUNCILMEMBER, WHO IS ALSO A LIFE
INSURANCE AGENT.

COUNCILMEMBER RECEIVES FEES IN EXCESS OF $250.00 ANNUALLY.

BECAUSE OF HIS RELATIONSHIP WITH MS. MUNOZ, AN EMPLOYEE OF SPECIALTY
VEHICLES, IS THERE A CONFLICT OF INTEREST?

PLEASE REVIEW THESE FACTS AND ADVISE ME AS TO WHETHER OR NOT YOU
BELIEVE A CONFLICT OF INTEREST EXISTS.

THANK YOU FOR YOU COOPERATION IN THIS MATTER.

SINCERELY,

RICHARD JONES
CITY ATTORNEY

[signature] Bob Allen

BOB ALLEN

LAW CLERK TO MR. JONES

VICTOR A. LOPEZ        THOMAS K. SAWYER        GENE H. CHANDLER        MYRON D. CLAXTON        ROBERT F. WOERRMANN        THOMAS G. MAULK
MAYOR                  MAYOR PRO TEM                COUNCILMAN                COUNCILMAN                COUNCILMAN                CITY MANAGER

PAGE.003

In response to your questions, the following is offered: Ms. Munoz maintains no ownership interest in Speciality Vehicles. Speciality Vehicles was the low bidder. By law, unless they are an unqualified bidder, we are required to accept the lowest bid, therefore Speciality Vehicles will receive the contract. At this point in time, we are unsure if Ms. Munoz receives a commission or will receive a commission as a result of this transaction. We will attempt to make that determination. Thank You for your professional assistance. Sincerely, Richard Jones City Attorney Bob Allen Law Clerk to Mr. Jones **CITY OF WHITTIER** 13230 PENN STREET, WHITTIER, CALIFORNIA 90602-1772 (213) 945-8200 TO BE FAXED 916-327-2026 JENNIFER FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION SACRAMENTO, CALIF November 14, 1990 Dear Jennifer: Per our conversation a few minutes ago regarding the question of whether or not a conflict of interest exist, the facts are as follows: The City of Whittier will at some point in the future award a bid to purchase five (05) trolleys for use in the rapid transit program. The lowbidder thus far is a company called Specialty Vehicles. Council will be asked in the near future to vote on approval of that contract and award to the low bidder the contract to purchase from them the five (05) vehicles. The sales representative for the company is a Ms. Nancy Munoz who has been assigned this account by the company. Ms. Munoz also purchases insurance from a councilmember, who is also a life insurance agent. Councilmember receives fees in excess of $250.00 annually. Because of his relationship with Ms. Munoz, an employee of Specialty Vehicles, is there a conflict of interest? Please review these facts and advise me as to whether or not you believe a conflict of interest exists. Thank you for your cooperation in this matter. Sincerely, Richard Jones City Attorney [Signature] Bob Allen Law Clerk to Mr. Jones
